summary: SLA Tracker feature testing complete. All 8 backend API endpoints pass (22/22 pytest tests). Frontend UI verified - SLA Tracker tab renders correctly with 4 sub-views (Dashboard, At-Risk CVEs, Escalation Rules, SLA Trends), Run Escalations button works, all charts and countdown timers functional.
critical_code_review_comments: SLA Tracker tab correctly positioned between Governance and Notifications in tab bar
All 8 SLA API endpoints working: dashboard, at-risk, escalation-rules (GET/PUT), run-escalations, escalation-log, history, snapshot
Default escalation rules (L1-75%, L2-100%, L3-150%) auto-seeded on first access
Countdown timers show remaining/overdue hours with visual progress bars
Per-severity compliance cards show SLA hours, total CVEs, breached/warning counts
SLA Trends view renders compliance trend and open vs breached charts using Recharts
data-testid attributes properly applied for all interactive elements
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - tested with existing CVE data (14 open CVEs)
context_for_next_testing_agent: SLA Tracker feature complete. All backend APIs tested via pytest. Frontend verified via Playwright - SLA Tracker tab accessible at /cve-management, shows 4 sub-views with live data. Run Escalations creates notifications based on at-risk CVEs.

api_endpoints_tested.GET /api/cve/sla/at-risk: PASS - Returns at-risk CVEs with countdown data
api_endpoints_tested.GET /api/cve/sla/escalation-rules: PASS - Returns 3 default rules (L1, L2, L3)
